question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user call getTopic() and receive the adminKey in the response, then use that public key material to identify the signing account and craft a social engineering attack against the topic owner without their authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a signer check in getTopic() to retrieve the submitKey of a topic and compare it against known public keys, identifying the authorized submitters without any of those signers' approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user call getTopic() with a realm-only format (realm.num) path variable, exploiting the EntityIdNumParameter parsing logic to resolve a topic ID under a different realm than intended, accessing topic data without the realm owner's authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdNumParameter.valueOf() parsing to supply a numeric-only ID that resolves to a different shard/realm combination than the attacker intends, causing getTopic() to return data for a topic the attacker should not be able to access without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user call getTopic() and receive the full Topic response including the feeScheduleKey without any ownership verification, learning which key controls fee updates and using that to plan unauthorized fee schedule manipulation without the key holder's approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unauthenticated getTopic() to retrieve the feeExemptKeyList and identify which accounts are exempt from paying custom fees, then impersonate those accounts in fee-related transactions without the exempt key holders' approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user call getTopic() and receive the autoRenewAccountId mapped from entity.autoRenewAccountId, identifying the account that will be charged for topic renewal and targeting it for depletion attacks without the account owner's authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of any CSRF protection or origin validation in getTopic() to trigger cross-origin requests that retrieve topic key material on behalf of an authenticated user, bypassing the requirement for signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user call getTopic() and receive the Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * header in the response, confirming that any web origin can retrieve full topic key material via cross-origin requests without any signer or owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the public Cache-Control: public, max-age=5 header returned by getTopic() to cause a caching proxy to serve stale topic key material to other users, enabling unauthorized access to sensitive topic configuration without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the public caching of getTopic() responses to poison a shared cache with a crafted response, causing other users to receive incorrect topic key material and authorizing transactions based on false key data without the real owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user call getTopic() for a topic with a keyList adminKey and receive the complete list of public keys, enabling them to identify all co-signers required for admin operations and target individual signers without the group's collective approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unauthenticated getTopic() to retrieve the threshold value of a threshold-key adminKey, learning the minimum number of signers required to authorize admin transactions and planning a targeted compromise of exactly that many signers without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user call getTopic() and receive the full Topic model without any rate limiting, enabling them to perform high-frequency enumeration of topic IDs to harvest key material at scale without any signer or owner authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unauthenticated getTopic() to confirm the existence of a topic before submitting unauthorized messages, using the 404 vs 200 response difference to oracle-attack topic existence without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user call getTopic() and receive the customFees including allCollectorsAreExempt flag without any ownership check, learning whether fee collectors are exempt and exploiting that information to craft fee-bypass transactions without the topic owner's authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unauthenticated getTopic() to retrieve the full custom fee structure including fixedFee denominatingTokenId, enabling them to identify the token used for fees and plan token-based attacks against the fee mechanism without the fee collector's approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user call getTopic() and receive the entity's timestampRange mapped via topicMapper.map(), learning the exact creation and modification timestamps to correlate with network transaction logs and reconstruct the signing history without owner authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unauthenticated getTopic() to retrieve the createdTimestamp mapped from entity.createdTimestamp, using it to identify the block/transaction that created the topic and extract the original signer's identity without their approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user call getTopic() and receive the full Topic response including all key fields without any proof of identity, effectively authorizing themselves to read the complete cryptographic configuration of any topic and plan unauthorized transactions against it?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unauthenticated getTopic() to retrieve the submitKey of a topic and compare it against a known set of public keys, confirming which accounts are authorized to submit messages and targeting those accounts for key compromise without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user call getTopic() with a shard.realm.num format ID and receive topic data for a topic in a specific shard/realm without any shard-level authorization check, bypassing assumed shard isolation without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unauthenticated getTopic() to retrieve the adminKey of a topic and use the public key to verify whether a given private key corresponds to the admin, enabling a targeted brute-force or side-channel attack without the admin's approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user call getTopic() and receive the full Topic response without any IP-based access control, enabling them to access sensitive topic configuration from any network location without owner or signer authorization?
